lesson: An ice machine that freezes into a solid block because it never initiates harvest is often caused by a failed curtain/harvest switch. If the switch reads closed constantly, the controller thinks the ice sheet is still on the plate.
symptoms: Ice machine is frozen into a single solid block of ice.
Compressor and water pump are running, but no ice is harvested.
Machine is making a grinding noise as the ice sheet hits the thick ice on the plate.

tests.inspect-beater: N/A. Evaporator plate is completely encased in a thick, solid block of ice that has bridged across the grid.
tests.continuity-test: Harvest switch (curtain switch) reads closed circuit (0 ohms) regardless of whether the curtain is open or closed. The magnetic reed switch has failed closed.

flavour: A new switch to close the curtain on this ice age.
authenticityNote: Ice machines detect when ice has fallen off the evaporator plate during harvest using a plastic curtain with a magnet and a reed switch. If the reed switch fails closed (magnetised or shorted), the machine will keep freezing indefinitely, eventually creating a solid freeze-up block.
